reforms.description: Denmark is implementing measures to encourage timely completion of tertiary education, including making it easier for students to transition from secondary to tertiary education and providing universities with incentives to promote on-time completion. Additionally, the government has been working towards increasing the share of public research grants allocated on a competitive basis, aiming for at least 50% of funds to be subject to open competition, alongside investments in R&D in the agriculture sector to support green growth.
reforms.component_name: Tertiary Education and R&D Funding Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging timely completion of education and increasing competitive research funding are expected to enhance human capital and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into practice as part of ongoing government initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms are significant but not structural, focusing on enhancing education and competitive funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The government reached agreement with its parliamentary partners in June 2009 on a 'green growth' initiative with new measures focused on improving the environmental performance of the agriculture sector.

reforms.description: The Danish government announced a 'green transport' initiative involving DKK94 billion in infrastructure spending over 2009-2020, aimed at upgrading rail transport and reducing car use.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in transport infrastructure is expected to enhance connectivity and efficiency, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been actively pursued and funded since its announcement.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a major investment in infrastructure with significant implications for the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced a 'green transport' initiative to follow up on a transport policy framework announced in December 2008.

reforms.description: The Danish government created a DKK90 billion fund to finance infrastructure projects over 2009-2020, addressing a significant infrastructure backlog and prioritizing investments based on socio-economic returns.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This fund aims to improve infrastructure, which is crucial for economic development and efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been operational since its creation, financing various infrastructure projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a large fund for infrastructure is a significant reform with broad economic implications.
reforms.source_quote: The government created a DKK90 billion fund to finance infrastructure projects over 2009-20, including in environment and public transport.

reforms.description: The Danish government announced investments of an additional €1.5 billion in R&D over the period of 2007-2010 to boost research and innovation activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation and competitiveness in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and utilized for various R&D initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This significant investment in R&D is a major reform aimed at enhancing the country's innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced investments of an additional €1.5 billion in R&D over 2007-10.

reforms.description: The Danish government unveiled a new package of reforms aimed at improving conditions for starting and operating businesses, which includes measures to facilitate access to authorities and streamline administrative processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the business environment is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform package represents a significant overhaul of the business operating environment.
reforms.source_quote: In March 2009, the government unveiled a new package of reforms to that effect, including providing better conditions for starting and operating businesses.

reforms.description: A new three-year collective employment agreement for the manufacturing sector was negotiated in 2007, which includes a commitment to employer-financed vocational training, allowing employees to receive training while earning a portion of their wages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Employer-financed vocational training is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, contributing to innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The agreement has been put into practice and is currently active.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this agreement is more of an incremental adjustment rather than a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: A new three-year collective employment agreement for the manufacturing sector... contained a new commitment to employer-financed vocational training.
